definitions.example: Many farmers were forced to abandon their land due to the prolonged drought.
She danced with reckless abandon at the festival.

abandon.etymology: ab-（离开）+ bandon（控制），原意「不再控制」，引申为「放弃」

similar_words.description: abundant 意为「丰富的」，与 abandon 拼写相近但词义无关

definitions.example: The patient complained of a sharp pain in the lower abdomen.

abdomen.etymology: 源自拉丁语 abdomen，意为「腹部」

similar_words.description: abdominal 是其形容词形式，意为「腹部的」

definitions.example: All members must abide by the rules of the organization.
He abided in the village for many years.

abide.etymology: 古英语 abidan，a-（强调）+ bidan（等待、停留）

similar_words.description: abode 为 abide 的过去式/过去分词，也可作名词「住所」
aside 意为「在一旁」，与 abide 拼写易混

definitions.example: His ability to solve complex problems impressed the interview panel.

ability.etymology: 源自拉丁语 habilitas（适合性），able 的名词形式

similar_words.description: agility 意为「敏捷」，与 ability 拼写相近

definitions.example: Scientists have detected abnormal climate patterns in recent decades.

similar_words.description: subnormal 意为「低于正常水平的」，前缀不同含义不同

definitions.meaning: 在（船、飞机、火车等）上；上（船、飞机等）
definitions.example: All passengers aboard the flight were evacuated safely.

similar_words.description: abroad 意为「在国外」，与 aboard 拼写极易混淆，需特别注意
broad 意为「宽广的」，与 aboard 词形相似

definitions.example: The government decided to abolish the outdated tax system.

abolish.etymology: 源自拉丁语 abolere（消灭），ab-（离开）+ olere（生长）

similar_words.description: demolish 意为「拆除、推翻」，含义相近但 abolish 多用于抽象事物
polish 意为「擦亮」，与 abolish 词尾相似

definitions.example: Opportunities abound in this rapidly developing region.

abound.etymology: 源自拉丁语 abundare（溢出），ab-（离开）+ undare（如波浪般涌出）

similar_words.description: abundant 是 abound 的形容词形式，意为「丰富的」
around 意为「在周围」，与 abound 拼写相近

definitions.example: The abrupt change in policy caught investors off guard.
His abrupt manner offended many of his colleagues.

abrupt.etymology: 源自拉丁语 abruptus，ab-（离开）+ rumpere（打破），意为「突然断开」

similar_words.description: corrupt 意为「腐败的」，同源于 rupt（破裂）
erupt 意为「爆发」，同根词

definitions.example: In the absence of clear evidence, the jury found him not guilty.

absence.etymology: ab-（离开）+ esse（存在），即「不在场」

similar_words.description: essence 意为「本质」，与 absence 词尾相同
presence 是 absence 的反义词，意为「出席、存在」

definitions.example: Several key witnesses were absent from the hearing.
He gave an absent nod, his thoughts clearly elsewhere.

similar_words.description: absence 是 absent 的名词形式

definitions.meaning: 绝对的；完全的；无条件的
definitions.example: The dictator demanded absolute obedience from his subjects.

absolute.etymology: 源自拉丁语 absolutus（解开的），ab-（离开）+ solvere（解开），引申为「无限制的」

similar_words.description: obsolete 意为「过时的」，与 absolute 词形相近
resolute 意为「坚决的」，词尾相同

definitions.example: Plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere through photosynthesis.

definitions.meaning: 使全神贯注；吸引（注意力）
definitions.example: She was completely absorbed in the novel and ignored the noise around her.

absorb.etymology: ab-（离开）+ sorbere（吞咽），意为「吞下、吸入」

similar_words.description: adsorb 意为「吸附（表面吸附）」，与 absorb 仅一字之差但物理含义不同

definitions.example: Philosophers often deal with abstract concepts that are difficult to define.

definitions.meaning: 摘要；提取；抽取（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The author was asked to write an abstract of the research paper.

abstract.etymology: abs-（离开）+ tract（拉），意为「抽离出来」

similar_words.description: attract 同根词，意为「吸引」
extract 同根词，意为「提取」

definitions.example: It is absurd to expect immediate results from such a long-term project.

absurd.etymology: 源自拉丁语 absurdus（不和谐的），ab-（强调）+ surdus（聋的、听不见的）

similar_words.description: obscure 意为「模糊的」，含义有别但常一同考查

definitions.example: The region is blessed with an abundance of natural resources.

abundance.etymology: 源自 abound（大量存在）的名词形式

similar_words.description: abandonment 意为「放弃」，词形相近但含义无关

definitions.example: The country has abundant supplies of oil and natural gas.

abundant.etymology: 源自拉丁语 abundans（溢出的），同 abound

similar_words.description: redundant 意为「多余的」，词尾相同但含义偏负面

definitions.example: Officials who abuse their power should be held accountable.
The referee was subjected to verbal abuse from angry fans.

abuse.etymology: ab-（偏离）+ use（使用），即「错误使用」

similar_words.description: misuse 意为「误用」，语气较 abuse 轻

definitions.example: His academic achievements earned him a scholarship to a prestigious university.

definitions.meaning: 纯理论的；不切实际的（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: Whether the plan will work is now an academic question, since funding has been withdrawn.

academic.etymology: 源自希腊 Akademia（柏拉图讲学之地）

similar_words.description: academy 是其名词形式，意为「学院」

definitions.example: She was admitted to a prestigious military academy after graduation.

similar_words.description: academic 是其形容词形式

definitions.example: The new policy is expected to accelerate economic growth in rural areas.

similar_words.description: decelerate 是反义词，意为「减速」
exhilarate 意为「使兴奋」，词形相近

definitions.example: She speaks English with a strong French accent.
The speaker placed special accent on the importance of innovation.

accent.etymology: 源自拉丁语 accentus，ac-（向）+ canere（唱）

similar_words.description: ascent 意为「上升」，与 accent 拼写极易混淆
assent 意为「同意」，发音和拼写相近

definitions.example: She refused to accept the verdict and decided to appeal.

similar_words.description: except 意为「除……之外」，与 accept 极易混淆，注意词义对立
expect 意为「期待」，词形相近

definitions.example: Public acceptance of the new technology grew steadily over the decade.

similar_words.description: acceptation 是较少见的名词形式，意为「（词的）通常含义」

definitions.meaning: 通道；进入（或使用）的权利、机会
definitions.example: Students should have free access to the university library.
Users can access the database remotely with a secure password.

similar_words.description: assess 意为「评估」，与 access 拼写易混
excess 意为「过量」，词形相近

definitions.example: Fashion accessories such as scarves and belts can transform an outfit.
He was charged as an accessory to the robbery.

accessory.etymology: 源自 access + -ory，「附加于主体之上的」

similar_words.description: accessary 是 accessory 的旧拼写，多用于法律语境

definitions.example: The traffic accident on the highway caused a major delay.
I met my old friend by accident at the airport.

accident.etymology: ac-（向）+ cid（落下）+ -ent，「落到身上的事」

similar_words.description: incident 意为「事件」，多指有意义的事件，常与 accident 区分
occident 意为「西方」，词形相近

definitions.example: The discovery of penicillin was largely accidental.

similar_words.description: incidental 意为「附带的」，含义有别

definitions.example: The novel was widely acclaimed for its profound social insights.

acclaim.etymology: ac-（向）+ claim（呼喊），即「向某人欢呼」

similar_words.description: claim 意为「声称、要求」，是其词根
exclaim 意为「惊呼」，同根词
proclaim 意为「宣告」，同根词

definitions.example: The new hotel can accommodate up to 500 guests at a time.

definitions.meaning: 适应；迁就；满足（需求）（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: Companies must accommodate the changing demands of consumers to survive.

similar_words.description: commodity 意为「商品」，同根词

definitions.example: The university provides accommodation for first-year students on campus.

definitions.meaning: 迁就；调和；妥协（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: An accommodation was reached between the two parties after long negotiations.

similar_words.description: 动词形式

definitions.example: Heavy rain was accompanied by strong winds throughout the night.

accompany.etymology: ac-（向）+ company（同伴），即「陪伴某人」

similar_words.description: company 意为「公司、陪伴」，是其词根

definitions.example: She accomplished what most people thought was impossible within just one year.

similar_words.description: complete 意为「完成」，同根词
complement 意为「补充」，词形相近

definitions.meaning: 一致；协议（of one's own accord 主动地）
definitions.example: The two countries reached an accord on trade and security issues.

definitions.meaning: 给予；授予；与……一致（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The committee accorded him the honor of opening the conference.

similar_words.description: according 意为「依据」，同根词
record 意为「记录」，词形相近

definitions.meaning: 一致；遵照（in accordance with 依据，按照）
definitions.example: All employees must act in accordance with company regulations.

similar_words.description: accord 是其词根，意为「一致」

definitions.example: The weather forecast predicts heavy rain, so dress accordingly.

similar_words.description: according to 意为「根据」，是常用短语

definitions.example: She gave a detailed account of the events that led to the accident.

definitions.meaning: 解释；说明；（在数量、比例上）占（account for）（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: Renewable energy now accounts for nearly 30 percent of the nation's electricity supply.

account.etymology: ac-（向）+ count（计算），即「报账、清算」

similar_words.description: accountant 意为「会计」
count 意为「计数」，是词根

definitions.example: The accountant prepared the company's annual financial report.

similar_words.description: account 是其词根

definitions.example: Over the years he managed to accumulate a vast collection of rare books.

accumulate.etymology: ac-（向）+ cumul（堆，参 cumulus 积云）+ -ate

similar_words.description: cumulative 意为「累积的」，同根词

definitions.example: Scientists are constantly improving the accuracy of weather forecasts.

accuracy.etymology: 源自拉丁语 accuratus（精心制作的），ac-（向）+ cura（关心、细心）

similar_words.description: accurate 是其形容词形式

definitions.example: The accurate measurement of temperature is crucial in chemical experiments.

similar_words.description: 注意 accurate（精确）与 adequate（足够）拼写相近
adequate 意为「足够的」，常被混淆

definitions.meaning: 指控；控告；指责（accuse sb. of sth.）
definitions.example: He was accused of leaking confidential information to the press.

accuse.etymology: ac-（向）+ cause（理由），即「向某人提出指控理由」

similar_words.description: excuse 意为「原谅、借口」，与 accuse 词形相近但含义相反

definitions.example: It took him a long time to accustom himself to the cold climate of the north.

similar_words.description: custom 意为「习俗、习惯」，是词根
customer 意为「顾客」，同根词

definitions.meaning: 习惯了的；通常的（be accustomed to doing）
definitions.example: She was accustomed to working long hours under pressure.

similar_words.description: customary 意为「习惯上的、惯例的」，含义相近

definitions.example: After the long hike, his legs ached terribly the next morning.

definitions.meaning: 渴望；极想（ache for/to do）（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: The lonely traveler ached to see his family again.

similar_words.description: 注意 headache、stomachache 等复合词

definitions.example: Through hard work and perseverance, she achieved remarkable success in her career.

similar_words.description: achievement 是其名词形式，意为「成就」
archive 意为「档案」，与 achieve 拼写相近

definitions.example: Acid rain has caused significant damage to forests across Europe.

definitions.meaning: 尖刻的；讽刺的（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: Her acid remarks made everyone at the table uncomfortable.

acid.etymology: 源自拉丁语 acidus（酸的，尖锐的）

similar_words.description: acidic 是其形容词形式，意为「酸性的」
arid 意为「干旱的」，与 acid 拼写相近

definitions.example: The government finally acknowledged the seriousness of the environmental crisis.

definitions.meaning: （对收到的信件、礼物等）告知收悉（熟词僻义）
definitions.example: Please acknowledge receipt of this letter at your earliest convenience.

acknowledge.etymology: ac-（向）+ knowledge（知道），即「承认知道」

similar_words.description: knowledge 意为「知识」，是词根

definitions.meaning: 使熟悉；使了解（acquaint sb. with sth.）
definitions.example: New employees should acquaint themselves with the company's regulations.

similar_words.description: acquaintance 是其名词形式，意为「熟人；相识」
quaint 意为「古雅的、奇特有趣的」，词形相近但含义不同

definitions.example: He has a wide circle of acquaintances in the academic community.

similar_words.description: 动词形式

definitions.meaning: 获得；取得；学到（知识、技能）
definitions.example: Children acquire language skills naturally through daily interaction.

definitions.meaning: 购得（公司、资产等）（商业用语）
definitions.example: The corporation acquired three smaller firms last year.

similar_words.description: require 意为「要求」，同根词
inquire 意为「询问」，同根词

definitions.example: Language acquisition in early childhood is a complex cognitive process.

similar_words.description: inquisition 意为「审讯、调查」，词形相近

definitions.meaning: 英亩（土地面积单位，约 4047 平方米）
definitions.example: The farmer owns over 200 acres of fertile land in the valley.

similar_words.description: acrid 意为「刺鼻的、辛辣的」，与 acre 拼写相近

definitions.example: The acrobat performed a series of daring stunts on the high wire.

acrobat.etymology: 希腊语 akro-（高、顶端）+ bat-（行走），即「在高处行走的人」

similar_words.description: aristocrat 意为「贵族」，词形相近但含义无关
